items.summary: PYMNTS published a feature titled 'Merchants Start Chasing High Approvals Instead of Low Fees' — EPX/North exec quoted: 'For a larger merchant, a one or two percent improvement in approval rates can add millions of dollars in revenue a year.' Merchants are now treating payment optimization as a revenue driver, not a cost center. The article highlights 'active vaulting' and AI-guided transaction routing as the 2026 frontier. This is direct mainstream validation of Revaly's RTN/approval optimization thesis and talking points.

Chargebacks911 CTO Donald Kossmann published in Help Net Security: agentic AI creates 'intent drift' — agents act within permissions but produce outcomes users reject, creating a dispute gray zone existing controls weren't designed for. Chargebacks911 is now getting both CEO and CTO published in security and fintech press, cementing authority in agentic dispute category. Incoming Revaly board member Brad Wiskerchen comes from this company — Revaly could leverage this IP and positioning directly.

Vindicia featured prominently in multiple 2026 subscription billing platform roundups, with Vindicia Select described as 'an AI-driven service specifically designed to recover failed payment transactions.' No new product announcements, but continued strong category presence through SEO-optimized comparison content. Vindicia's market positioning remains focused on high-volume digital media — an overlapping segment with Revaly's publishing/media wins like AdvantageCS.

Finsi.ai published a 'Complete Guide to Failed Payment Recovery' targeting subscription businesses — covering decline codes, smart retry strategies, email sequences, and ROI calculation. Finsi.ai appears to be a newer AI-native entrant entering the recovery content/product space. Worth monitoring as an emerging competitor building inbound SEO presence in Revaly's core category.

Nacha ACH mandate takes effect March 20, 2026 — Payscout and others actively advising merchants to ensure ACH workflows are as secure and compliant as card processing. Combined with the Visa subscription mandate taking effect April 18, Q1/Q2 2026 is a compliance-heavy period for subscription merchants. Increased compliance pressure often triggers merchant evaluation of billing and recovery stack — a window for Revaly outreach.

Mastercard's Recorded Future annual fraud report (published week of Mar 10) states winning organizations will 'align leadership, fuse cyber and fraud intelligence, and maximize signal detection across fragmented data sources.' Cross-network signal sharing is now an explicit Mastercard-endorsed best practice — third consecutive week this theme surfaces in major press. Directly validates Revaly's RTN/issuer connectivity architecture as the emerging industry standard.

Tuhk named one of 10 Canadian fintech startups to watch in 2026 by Fintech.ca. Described as building a collaborative payments platform enabling real-time data sharing between merchants, banks, and service providers to combat fraud — directly competing with Revaly's RTN bi-directional data rails thesis. Tuhk's approach mirrors Revaly's core moat: network-level trust signals shared upstream. Worth monitoring for overlap with Revaly's issuer partnerships and RTN narrative.
items.source: Fintech.ca — Canadian Fintech Startups to Watch 2026

items.summary: Chargebacks911 CEO Monica Eaton quoted in mainstream fintech press (Finextra): 'The card wasn't stolen. The merchant didn't make a mistake. The agent did exactly what it was told.' Chargebacks911 is actively publishing content and briefing media on the agentic AI chargeback wave, reinforcing their positioning as the authority on emerging dispute categories. Brad Wiskerchen (incoming Revaly board member) brings this IP network directly to Revaly's board.

Kount (Equifax) continuing to be cited in 2026 fraud prevention roundups specifically for eCommerce and subscription-based businesses — framed as 'improving customer trust by preventing chargebacks and reducing payment fraud.' Kount's positioning increasingly overlaps with Revaly's approval optimization layer as Equifax-backed resources allow them to scale their identity graph. No new product announcements, but market citation frequency increasing.

A new crypto gateway platform is actively promoting its Vindicia integration in mainstream sponsored content as a solution to 'reduce subscription losses and streamline recovery of failed transactions.' Vindicia is extending its recovery surface into crypto and digital wallet fallback rails — a tactical expansion to reduce card-based failure exposure. No major strategic shift, but continued market presence in the recovery category.

Multiple independent industry sources in March 2026 cite 20-40% involuntary churn recovery rates as industry benchmark for AI-powered dunning flows. Subscription Commerce 2026 guide and G2's AI churn reduction expert survey both anchor this range, legitimizing Revaly's core recovery value proposition to a broader buyer audience. The narrative is mainstream — prospect education cost is decreasing for Revaly.

Mastercard's 2026 annual payment fraud intelligence report (with Recorded Future) explicitly states the winning organizations will 'fuse cyber and fraud intelligence and maximize signal detection across fragmented data sources.' Quote from Ilya Volovik. This is the third consecutive week Mastercard is cited as validating the cross-network signal-sharing architecture that powers Revaly's RTN product.
items.source: Mastercard.com — Recorded Future Annual Payment Fraud Report 2026

items.summary: Customer Experience Dive published a feature on Butter Payments, citing their stat that involuntary churn costs subscription companies up to $440B annually. Butter claims 128 unique data points per transaction and 10% ARR improvement for clients. This is mainstream B2B press — not a trade pub — reaching CX and ops buyers who haven't traditionally been in the recovery category. Butter is actively expanding its buyer audience beyond payments teams.

Revaly's Baily Dyer announced the company's first 'Peers in Payments' webinar series for 2026 via LinkedIn (posted 5 days ago). Signals Revaly is investing in community-led marketing and thought leadership events — a category-building move that could accelerate brand awareness in the payments vertical alongside the AdvantageCS partnership win.

A payments industry commentary piece flagged that the Federal Reserve's March 2026 technical incident (ongoing, full scope still being assessed) has caused real payment failures for small businesses — payroll delayed, vendor payments bounced, customer transactions failed. Systemic infrastructure fragility is creating a new wave of involuntary payment failures unrelated to card-level issues, a category Revaly's recovery engine is well-positioned to address.

AdvantageCS (subscription management platform for publishers and membership orgs) announced a formal integration with Revaly for intelligent payment recovery. When a digital payment fails, Advantage routes it to Revaly for recovery guidance and optimal retry timing. Quote from Matt Varblow: 'By combining Advantage's industry-proven subscription capabilities with Revaly's recovery expertise, we help publishers and membership organizations keep more subscribers active month after month.' Signals Revaly is winning enterprise channel partnerships in publishing/media vertical.

The Payments Association published a feature on payment orchestration as the dominant 2026 strategy to 'turn declines into approvals.' Market narrative around approval optimization is now mainstream — multiple vendors (Paysecure and others) explicitly pitching acceptance rate uplift as the primary value prop. This validates Revaly's RTN thesis but also means more incumbents entering the upfront approval space.

Visa subscription management and cardholder controls mandate takes effect April 18, 2026 — deadline for all subscription merchants. Requires enhanced merchant data, DBA name, address, phone, website at transaction level. Enhanced merchant data (enhanced descriptor) deadline follows Jan 2027. Tighter compliance requirements increase switching friction for merchants already embedded in billing platforms.

Butter Payments is expanding beyond recovery into full customer retention — new 'Payments Score' product predicts likelihood of payment before recovery even starts, and 'Outreach' product uses ML to determine optimal channel (email/SMS/silent/call) per failed transaction. CEO explicitly positioning Butter to solve customer acquisition costs and retention, not just failed payments. Direct encroachment on Revaly's RTN up-front approval thesis.

Chargebacks911 published: agentic commerce creates a new dispute 'gray zone' — AI agents operating within permissions but producing outcomes users reject, creating chargebacks that existing fraud controls weren't built for. Chargebacks911 is actively positioning to own this emerging category. Visa already completing secure AI-initiated transactions with partners. Revaly's approval optimization thesis has a new validation vector here.
